About Us:

Blackbook.ai is an Australian founded company specialising in Artificial Intelligence, Automation, DataOps, Digital and Analytics. We are passionate about helping organisations unlock their true value, with a proven track record across more than 180 successful client engagements.

Focused on leveraging and nurturing local talent, we provide Australian companies with access to skilled consultants who deliver innovative solutions to complex business challenges. Headquartered in Brisbane with additional offices in Sydney and Melbourne, Blackbook.ai now operates across more than 8 cities globally, collaborating with prominent organisations to drive efficiency, excellence, and measurable outcomes through cutting-edge technology.

About the Team Your are Joining:

You'll be joining our GenAI and Applied AI team. Our focus is building production AI solutions for large Australian enterprises and government clients, with most of our work centred on GenAI and applied AI: RAG systems, document intelligence, agents, and LLM-backed workflows that need to hold up under real data volumes, security requirements, and operational load. We take AI from prototype to production. That means hardening, scaling, deploying, and handing over systems clients can actually run, along with greenfield delivery end-to-end when clients bring us the problem rather than the POC.

About You:

Senior, hands-on delivery role within the GenAI / Applied AI team, leading production GenAI work for large Australian enterprise and government clients. You'd architect, build, and deploy RAG systems, agents, document intelligence, and LLM-backed workflows — typically taking a POC/MVP to production (security, scale, observability, evaluation) or leading greenfield delivery end-to-end, while owning the client relationship through scoping, build, and handover. Also mentors juniors and contributes to internal patterns/accelerators.

Must have: 5+ years across SWE/data/ML/AI with the last 12–24 months in production GenAI; strong Python + API/service dev; production experience on Azure and/or AWS; consulting delivery chops (scoping, workshops, stakeholder management); daily use of AI coding tools; CS/SWE/DS degree or equivalent; full Australian working rights.

Nice to have: regulated-industry delivery, Databricks/GCP, agent frameworks (LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, AutoGen), eval-led development, full-stack/DevOps breadth.

Location: Brisbane preferred; Sydney/Melbourne considered. Some travel to client sites.

What We Offer:

Applied AI work with large Australian enterprises and government clients, on problems that go into production.

A team that takes production quality seriously: testing, evaluation, observability, and proper handover.

Direct client engagement. You'll be working with clients, not relaying tickets.

Investment in certifications (Azure, AWS, Databricks) and time for internal R&D.

Competitive remuneration, commensurate with experience.

Flexible working conditions and a collaborative, technically strong team.

Free Spotify Accounts

50% off Gym memberships

Additional leave entitlements after 2 years

Job Description

The Azure Data Solutions Architect leads the design and implementation of enterprise-scale data solutions using Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, and Azure Synapse Analytics. This role provides architectural leadership, ensuring solutions are robust, scalable, and aligned with business strategy and industry best practices. The architect drives innovation, enforces governance, and mentors' teams to deliver high-quality, future-ready data platforms that support organizational transformation.




Key Responsibilities

1. Provide Architectural Leadership by Designing End-To-End Data Integration and Analytics Solutions Using Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, And Azure Synapse Analytics, Ensuring Alignment with Business Objectives and Compliance with Architectural Standards.


2. Define And Enforce Governance, Security, And Compliance Frameworks for Azure Data Solutions, Leveraging Azure Devops for Automated Deployment, Monitoring, And Quality Assurance.


3. Collaborate With Business Stakeholders to Gather Requirements and Translate Them into Scalable Azure Data Architectures, Optimizing Data Pipelines and Workflows for Performance and Reliability.


4. Review And Validate Architecture and Design Deliverables, Ensuring Adherence to Best Practices in Azure Data Engineering and Continuous Improvement of Solution Quality.


5. Drive Knowledge Upgradation by Evaluating Emerging Azure Technologies, Establishing Standards, And Mentoring Teams on Advanced Data Engineering Practices and Platform Capabilities.


6. Lead The Development and Implementation of Reusable Data Assets, Frameworks, And Accelerators to Streamline Delivery and Ensure Consistency Across Projects.




Skill Requirements

1. Architectural Leadership in Azure Data Factory (Adf), Azure Databricks, And Azure Synapse Analytics, With Expert Ability to Design and Govern Complex Data Solutions.


2. Advanced Proficiency in Azure Devops for Ci/Cd Automation, InfrastructureAsCode, And Deployment of Data Pipelines.


3. Excellent Understanding of Data Modeling, Etl/Elt Processes, Data Governance, And Security Within the Azure Ecosystem.


4. Strong Expertise in Designing Scalable, High-performance Data Architectures for Analytics and Reporting.


5. Solid Knowledge of Industry Best Practices for Cloud Data Platforms, Including Compliance, Monitoring, And Optimization




Other Requirements

1. Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert (Recommended)
2. Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate (Optional But Valuable

The role

Passionate about leading high‑impact machine learning initiatives and delivering scalable, production‑ready AI solutions?

Motivated to mentor and grow a team of ML and data engineers while remaining hands‑on with advanced model development and deployment?

Inspired to join a collaborative Data & AI organisation that values innovation, technical excellence, and continuous learning?

As our new Machine Learning Engineer – Team Lead, you will play a pivotal role in leading the design and delivery of machine learning solutions end‑to‑end, while providing technical leadership and mentorship to a small, high‑performing engineering team. You will bridge business objectives with advanced ML techniques, guide architectural decisions, and ensure models are production‑ready, scalable, and continuously improved.

Key responsibilities will include:

Lead and mentor a team of ML and Data Engineers, providing technical guidance, regular feedback, and supporting professional development.
Own the end‑to‑end lifecycle of machine learning initiatives, translating business requirements into well‑defined ML problem statements, technical solutions, and delivery plans.
Design, build, train, and evaluate advanced machine learning models, and contribute to architectural decisions for scalable ML pipelines and cloud‑based deployments (Azure preferred).
Partner with MLOps and DevOps teams to implement CI/CD pipelines, model versioning, monitoring, retraining, and production support best practices.
Collaborate cross‑functionally with Data Engineering, Software Development, Sales & Pre‑Sales, and leadership stakeholders, while staying current with emerging ML technologies and driving adoption where valuable.

What we need to see from you
Proven experience delivering machine learning solutions into production environments.
Strong technical expertise in Python and ML frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Scikit‑learn.
Solid understanding of end‑to‑end ML pipelines, data preprocessing, feature engineering, model evaluation, and monitoring.
Experience working with cloud platforms (Azure preferred), Docker, Kubernetes, and MLOps tooling (e.g., MLflow, Airflow).
Ability to balance technical depth with leadership responsibilities and commercial awareness and having experience working within agile delivery environments and across multiple stakeholder groups

Job Function

Software & Cloud
